heading: The Talk of the Town
description: The New Yorker's Talk of the Town, fetched verbatim via authenticated Jina reader. Traversed backwards in time — most recent first, each article featured once and never repeated.

covered.title: Pay Later
covered.author_or_venue: E. Tammy Kim / The New Yorker
covered.one_line_summary: E. Tammy Kim on deferred financial pain — buy-now-pay-later economy and its structural costs to working families.

covered.title: Bird Flu / Canada Geese piece
covered.one_line_summary: Talk of the Town piece on avian flu spread via migratory Canada geese — ecological anxiety in the urban-wild interface.

covered.title: When Soul Food Met Daniel Boulud
covered.one_line_summary: Zach Helfand on Charles Pan-Fried Chicken's monthly chef collaborations — Lana Lagomarsini's oxtails meet Harlem's soul food heritage.

covered.title: The Anatomy of a Failure
covered.one_line_summary: Paris Postcard on Flops?! at Musée des Arts et Métiers — inventions from spray-on condoms to radioactive wrinkle cream celebrated in their glorious failure.

covered.title: The Popes That Trump Might've Liked
covered.one_line_summary: Jane Bua surveys papal history for the tough-guy pontiffs Donald Trump might approve of — Urban VI, Stephen VI, Alexander VI.

covered.title: Donald Trump's Spring Cleaning
covered.one_line_summary: Wallace-Wells on Pam Bondi, Kristi Noem, Lori Chavez-DeRemer departures — general unseriousness inside the MAGA cabinet, months from the midterms.

covered.title: Reverend Billy Takes On Norman Foster's New Monolith
covered.one_line_summary: Street preacher Billy Talen's 24-hour Earth Day sermon at JPMorgan Chase's Norman Foster HQ, condemning fossil fuel ties — now touring as Neil Young's opening act.

covered.title: Medallions, Movement, and Mamdani at MOMA PS1
covered.one_line_summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Richard Chow — the cabdriver from Myanmar whose brother died under taxi-medallion debt — from hunger strike to City Hall.

covered.title: Patrick Ball's Path to Broadway and ‘Becky Shaw’
covered.one_line_summary: Patrick Ball (star of NBC's The Pitt) traces his road from medical drama to Gina Gionfriddo's Becky Shaw on Broadway.

covered.title: Ellen Burstyn's Inner Library
covered.one_line_summary: Burstyn, 93, on organizing a life around poetry — her anthology ‘Poetry Says It Better’ and poetry as knowledge that outlasts memory.
